description: Uniform active-surface routing (ADR 01081) on Windows: surface-sensitive steps that omit `surface` act on the most recently active surface — here the app that startSurface just opened — and an explicit `surface` reference switches the active surface for the surface-less steps that follow. The app-only test proves every surface-sensitive step kind (click string shorthand, find, type, swipe, screenshot) routes to the app with no `surface` on any step (pre-ADR these fell through to a browser path and failed); the mixed test proves the most-recently-active rule across kinds — browser → app → explicit back to browser → explicit back to app — and that closing the active surface falls through to the next live one. Character Map is the target app for the same reason as app-surfaces.spec.json (a real Win32 executable on every Windows edition).

tests.description: Launch Character Map, then drive it with ONLY surface-less steps: click by string shorthand (maps to the app's element text), type into the copy box, find the Copy button, swipe the character grid, and screenshot — none carry `surface`. Then reference the app explicitly once and follow with another surface-less find to prove the explicit reference persists as the active surface. No browser is provisioned for this test (the classification exclusion is part of the feature).

The most-recently-active rule across kinds: goTo makes the browser active (surface-less find hits the page), startSurface makes the app active (surface-less find hits the app), an explicit engine reference switches back to the browser (the surface-less follow-up stays there), an explicit app reference switches back to the app, and closing the app falls through to the browser — the next live surface — for the final surface-less find. Pinned to chrome so the engine-keyword reference matches the context's session.
